summary: AIGOS sits in an emerging GTM operations category where direct niche market sizing is not yet defensible from the available sources.
keyFindings: The defensible category frame is B2B SaaS GTM operations, not the broader CRM market.
Only parent-market sizing is available in this fixture, so it is labeled as TAM context.
assumptions: Competitor density will be validated in the research-competitor lane.

market_scope.buyer_context: Growth-stage SaaS companies that need clearer GTM execution.

category_definition.claim: GTM operations software coordinates research, strategy, media planning, and production around a locked company brief.
category_definition.evidence_quote: GTM teams need one operating layer for research, strategy, media planning, and scripts.
category_definition.definition: Software that coordinates go-to-market planning and execution for SaaS teams.

market_size_signals.label: tam_context
market_size_signals.value: Parent-market figure only; not direct niche size.
caveats: Broader parent market, not direct GTM operations SAM.

category_maturity.claim: The category is emerging because buyers describe the workflow more often than the category name.
category_maturity.evidence_quote: Teams search for planning, research, media plan, and script workflows separately.

timing_signals.claim: AI workflow adoption is increasing interest in GTM automation.
timing_signals.evidence_quote: SaaS teams are adopting AI workflows for research and planning.

demand_drivers.claim: Small GTM teams need faster research-to-campaign handoff.
demand_drivers.evidence_quote: Lean teams are compressing research, planning, and production cycles.

buying_triggers.claim: A new growth leader often triggers a structured GTM planning reset.

adoption_barriers.claim: Teams distrust unsupported AI-generated market claims.

primary.claim: Research, media planning, and scripts often live in disconnected tools.

caveats: Competitor lane owns direct competitor verification.

opportunity_candidates.claim: Evidence-first GTM planning is a visible gap for lean SaaS teams.

source_gaps.reason: No direct GTM operations SAM source was available in the fixture.
attempted_queries: B2B SaaS GTM operations market size

categorySnapshot.marketSize: TAM context: Parent-market figure only; not direct niche size.

primary: Research, media planning, and scripts often live in disconnected tools.

demandDrivers: Small GTM teams need faster research-to-campaign handoff.
buyingTriggers: A new growth leader often triggers a structured GTM planning reset.
barriersToPurchase: Teams distrust unsupported AI-generated market claims.

trendSignals.evidence: SaaS teams are adopting AI workflows for research and planning.

summaryRecommendations: Lead with evidence-backed planning rather than generic automation.

marketOpportunities.evidence: Evidence-first GTM planning is a visible gap for lean SaaS teams.
